skill.name: azure-maps-search-dotnet
skill.description: Guidance for using Azure Maps SDK clients in .NET applications. Covers geocoding, routing, rendering, IP geolocation, weather, authentication, and error handling.
skill.summary: Build .NET location features with Azure Maps SDK examples and workflow guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found documentation examples, Azure reference links, and textual false positives, with no auto-executing scripts or prompt injection. Low residual risk remains because users may copy commands that install packages, call Azure services, or write map tiles to disk.

low_findings.title: Documentation Commands Flagged as External Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The detected command text is inside Markdown examples for dotnet package installation. The skill does not include executable scripts that run these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear only in fenced Markdown and a reference table. No wrapper script, hook, or instruction causes automatic execution.

low_findings.title: Azure Reference URLs Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs are documentation, API reference, GitHub source, and pricing links. They are not contacted by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are in a Reference Links table and point to Microsoft or Azure documentation. No code sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported lines contain front matter text, route destination sample data, and a routing type table. No weak cryptographic algorithm is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each reported line found no hashing, encryption, signing, or crypto API usage. The matches are substring artifacts.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Match Is Error Handling Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported line is a comment for HTTP 400 invalid request handling. It does not inspect the host system or enumerate local resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears in a C# RequestFailedException switch example. There is no command or API call for system discovery.

low_findings.title: Map Tile Example Writes a Local File
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The rendering example saves a map tile stream to ./MapTile.png. This is expected sample behavior, but users should review output paths before copying it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The File.Create call is explicit and writes to a local relative path. The risk is limited because it is documentation, not skill-executed code.

content.value_statement: Location features often require several Azure Maps SDK clients and authentication choices. This skill gives .NET guidance for geocoding, routing, rendering, IP geolocation, and weather.
actual_capabilities: Shows package installation commands for Azure Maps .NET SDK libraries.
Explains subscription key, Microsoft Entra ID, and SAS authentication options.
Provides examples for geocoding, reverse geocoding, and boundary polygons.
Covers routing workflows, including directions, route matrix, and isochrones.
Includes rendering, IP geolocation, weather, and error handling examples.
limitations: The skill is reference documentation, not a runnable Azure Maps client.
Examples require a valid Azure Maps account and credentials.
Several package examples use prerelease SDK versions listed in the file.
Users must verify pricing, quotas, and regional service availability.

use_cases.title: Add Address Search
use_cases.description: Use geocoding and reverse geocoding examples to add address lookup to a .NET application.

use_cases.title: Plan Route Features
use_cases.description: Compare routing examples for directions, route matrices, travel modes, and traffic-aware estimates.

use_cases.title: Prototype Map Services
use_cases.description: Review rendering, IP geolocation, and weather examples before building a location service prototype.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Client
prompt_templates.prompt: I need Azure Maps in a .NET app. Recommend the SDK client for geocoding, routing, rendering, IP geolocation, and weather.

prompt_templates.title: Add Geocoding
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to add Azure Maps geocoding to my .NET service using Microsoft Entra ID and clear error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Design Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Help design a route planning feature with directions, traffic data, route matrix limits, and async processing guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure Maps .NET design for authentication, rate limits, caching, local file writes, and operational failure cases.

output_examples.input: Ask for the right Azure Maps client for address lookup and routing.
output_examples.output: A short recommendation that maps address search to MapsSearchClient and routing workflows to MapsRoutingClient.

output_examples.input: Ask how to add route matrix support for many origins and destinations.
output_examples.output: Guidance on immediate versus asynchronous route matrix usage, limits, traffic options, and response handling.

output_examples.input: Ask for production concerns before using map tiles and weather data.
output_examples.output: A review covering authentication, rate limits, caching, pricing checks, and safe local output paths.

best_practices: Prefer Microsoft Entra ID for production authentication when possible.
Cache geocoding and rendering results when policy and freshness needs allow it.
Handle rate limits, authentication failures, and invalid request errors explicitly.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode real Azure Maps subscription keys in source code.
Do not assume prerelease package APIs are stable across upgrades.
Do not write downloaded map assets to user-controlled paths without validation.

faq.question: Does this skill run Azure Maps commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples that users may copy into their own projects.

faq.question: Which Azure Maps authentication method is best for production?
faq.answer: Microsoft Entra ID is recommended when supported by the application and deployment environment.

faq.question: Can it help with both geocoding and reverse geocoding?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for address-to-coordinate and coordinate-to-address workflows.

faq.question: Does it cover route matrix calculations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes immediate route matrix guidance and asynchronous matrix guidance for larger workloads.

faq.question: Does it include map tile rendering?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to request a map tile and save the returned stream as a local image file.

faq.question: Are the listed package versions guaranteed current?
faq.answer: No. Users should verify current SDK versions before adding packages to a project.
